description: MRI Liver, with and without IV contrast (multiphase)
facility: University of Maryland Medical Center — Radiology
radiologist: Dr. Priya Nathan / Dr. Henry Cole (abdominal imaging)
indication: Characterization of the heterogeneous right-hepatic-lobe abnormality seen on CTPA (7/6).
technique: Multiphase contrast-enhanced MRI of the liver (T1 in/out of phase, T2, diffusion-weighted, dynamic post-gadolinium arterial/portal-venous/delayed phases).
findings: A large, 17 cm abnormality occupies much of the POSTERIOR RIGHT hepatic lobe, with heterogeneous signal and enhancement. Multiple ADDITIONAL areas of abnormal signal/enhancement are present in the LEFT lobe. There is apparent THROMBUS within branches of the RIGHT PORTAL VEIN adjacent to the dominant lesion. The lesions do not follow a simple benign pattern (not typical of hemangioma, FNH, or simple steatosis). No biliary dilatation. Spleen normal. No ascites.
impression:
1. Dominant 17 cm right-hepatic-lobe lesion with additional left-lobe foci and apparent right portal-vein thrombus — HIGH SUSPICION for malignancy (primary hepatic vs metastatic). The portal-vein thrombus may represent bland or tumor thrombus.
2. RECOMMENDATION: CONSIDER NEEDLE BIOPSY for tissue diagnosis.

(Note: the patient is concurrently anticoagulated for acute VTE — the timing/safety of any biopsy must be coordinated with the treating teams.)
comparison: Corresponds to the incidental finding flagged on the 7/6 CTPA; now far better characterized.
